items.title: Thailand SEC files complaint against Bitkub over alleged false disclosures
items.summary: Thailand’s SEC filed a criminal complaint against Bitkub and two former directors over alleged false disclosures linked to a 2021 cyberattack involving $50 million in assets.

items.title: Morning Minute: Bitcoin's New $15M Quantum Defense Fund
items.summary: Plus, the Clarity Act is no longer expected to progress before the August recess. And Vlad Tenev's X was hacked to post a meme coin...

items.title: India Orders GitHub to Take Down Jack Dorsey's Bitchat Amid Protests
items.summary: The order gave GitHub three hours to pull three code repositories, citing the messaging app's use during protest internet shutdowns.

items.title: Odos Protocol to shut down, gives users until July 30 to withdraw assets
items.summary: Odos Protocol will shut down on July 30, giving users one week to withdraw assets. The team did not provide a reason for the decision.

items.title: EU hits Russia with massive 21st sanctions package targeting $120B crypto network
items.summary: The EU is considering a ban on third-country crypto services providers for the first time and is targeting 14 crypto companies, which it has not named yet.

items.title: Crypto market maker B2C2 held sale talks with multiple potential buyers
items.summary: The SBI-owned crypto trading firm is said to have attracted takeover interest from a number of suitors over the past 18 months, but valuation has been a sticking point.

items.title: RWAs become Hyperliquid’s largest trading category
items.summary: Tokenized RWA trading became Hyperliquid’s largest trading category for the first time, accounting for more than half of the decentralized exchange’s weekly trading volume.

items.summary: Your day-ahead look for July 24, 2026

items.title: A $5 billion cluster has formed in bitcoin options. It tells a bullish story
items.summary: Bitcoin's $70,000 and $72,000 call strikes on Deribit have accumulated nearly $5 billion in open interest, with calls outnumbering puts by a significant margin.

items.title: Bitcoin settles near $65,000 as oil's march toward $100 fails to spook the market
items.summary: BTC held just under $65,000 and crypto broadly rose on Friday even as Brent crude surged to $97.66, its highest since May, as the Iran conflict persists and traditional markets stay muted.

items.title: Poolin was bitcoin's biggest mining pool and now it's filing for bankruptcy
items.summary: The Singapore-based company once controlled nearly a fifth of Bitcoin's global hashrate. Now it owes $173 million and is selling off what's left.

items.title: Bitcoin treasury companies sell up, repay debt, pivot to AI as share prices collapse
items.summary: Falling share prices, debt obligations and difficult market conditions are forcing former bitcoin accumulators to sell their holdings and restructure operations.

items.title: Brazil puts tokenized cows to work as loan collateral: Report
items.summary: Ten tokenized dairy cows backed a $19,600 loan registered on Brazil’s B3, in one of Brazil’s first uses of tokenized livestock as loan collateral.

items.title: India orders takedown of Jack Dorsey’s bitcoin-linked messaging app Bitchat
items.summary: The order targets Bitchat, an offline Bluetooth mesh app built to relay encrypted messages and bitcoin transactions, as 'Cockroach Janta Party' protesters in Delhi use mesh tools to communicate through internet shutdowns.

items.title: Samsung Wallet plans stablecoin support in digital payments expansion
items.summary: Samsung Electronics plans to add stablecoin support to Samsung Wallet, expanding its mobile payments and rewards platform to include digital assets.

items.title: Philippine bank BPI plans stablecoin payments pilot
items.summary: The Philippine bank is preparing a stablecoin settlement pilot intended to speed up and reduce the cost of overseas payments to Filipino remote workers.

items.title: BitMEX faces proposed class-action suit for theft, insider trading as crypto exchange shuts down
items.summary: The complaint claims BitMEX designed a system to retain customer collateral and alleges an internal desk accessed private user data during server freezes.

items.title: BTC supply in profit eyes 60%, but analysis hints recovery may ‘roll back over’
items.summary: Bitcoin supply profitability improved versus its 2026 low, but analysis warned that a fake recovery had already broken down at the start of June.

items.title: Polymarket Account in Farage Backer's Name Took $9M in Crypto From Unknown Sources: FT
items.summary: The "GCottrell93" account bet the funds on Trump's election win and cashed out the profit, though who paid in and who collected is unclear.

items.title: South Korea’s Korbit to rebrand as Digital X under Mirae: Report
items.summary: Mirae Asset reportedly plans to use the South Korean crypto exchange as a hub for tokenized assets, stablecoins and digital finance.

items.title: Ripple launches Mint to expand institutional access to RLUSD
items.summary: Ripple launches Mint to expand RLUSD access for institutions as the stablecoin’s market cap approaches $1.6 billion.

items.title: Bitcoin mining pool Poolin files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy
items.summary: Bitcoin miner Poolin filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and initiated a $52 million sale of its two West Texas mining sites as part of its future creditor recovery program.

items.title: EU widens Belarus ownership ban to all crypto service providers
items.summary: The EU will bar Belarusian nationals and residents from owning, controlling or managing exchanges and other MiCA-regulated crypto firms from Aug. 25.

items.title: Live updates: Bitcoin gives up gains, falls below $64,000 as stocks retreat
items.summary: Michael Saylor's Strategy — under continued pressure in this crypto bear market — released a new set of metrics to judge company performance.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs snap 7-day inflow streak with $225M in outflows
items.summary: After attracting nearly $1 billion over seven trading sessions, US-listed spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded their first daily net outflow since July 13.

items.title: Ripple’s RLUSD gets two boosts as transfer volume drops 25%
items.summary: Ripple launched an institutional minting platform and added RLUSD to Notabene’s compliance network as holder growth rose but monthly transfer volume fell to $10.95 billion.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Mirae plans to turn crypto exchange Korbit into something Korea hasn't seen before
items.summary: The South Korean financial giant says its Korbit – soon to be rebranded as Digital X – is just the starting point for a broader ecosystem that connects RWAs, stablecoins and digital assets.

items.summary: The proposed class action alleges BitMEX used privileged trading access and server freezes to profit from forced liquidations.

items.title: Bitcoin holds near $65,000 as $800 billion AI selloff leaves crypto largely untouched
items.summary: The Magnificent Seven had their worst day since April 2025 after Alphabet and Tesla spooked investors on AI spending. Bitcoin fell less than 1%, and dogecoin led the majors lower.

items.title: Dem senator calls GOP’s CLARITY ethics proposal a ‘piece of s---’: Politico
items.summary: On Wednesday, Senate Republicans released the proposed text for the CLARITY Act, which has been met with pushback from Democrats regarding ethics provisions.

items.summary: Nasdaq, Cboe and the London Stock Exchange are among major exchanges moving toward longer trading hours.

items.summary: FLUX 3 is the German AI lab’s first video model, and the same system is already teaching robots to work an Audi assembly line.

items.summary: The Bitcoin donation can be used to support Donald Trump as a New York court considers whether to reverse a $5 million settlement between the CFTC and Gemini.
